gaps: Pointhound has no documented public API; this CLI builds against the website's reverse-engineered /api/offers surface. If Pointhound changes that surface, regen via /printing-press-reprint will be required.

Search creation (POST /flights with base64-protobuf q parameter) is Cloudflare-gated and only works after `auth login --chrome`. Without it, the CLI can read offers using a searchId obtained from the website URL but cannot create new searches programmatically.

Supabase user-data RPCs (db.pointhound.com) are intentionally omitted from v1. Adding account/subscription/wallet commands requires extracting the Supabase JWT from Chrome's localStorage `ph_app` key.

The protobuf encoder for the search-create q parameter was reverse-engineered from one observed sample. The cabin enum (1=economy, 2=premium_economy, 3=business, 4=first inferred) may need updating if Pointhound changes the schema.

novel_features.name: Reachability from points balance
novel_features.description: Tell me where I can fly with the points I actually hold — feed in your Chase UR, Amex MR, Bilt, Capital One, and Citi TY balances and get back every destination reachable in the requested cabin within those balances, ranked by lowest effective spend.
novel_features.rationale: Joins offers × airlines × transferOptions against your balance map locally. Pointhound's own web UI can't ask this because it has no concept of 'your balance'.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent has a goal like 'plan a fall trip' and a balance fact like 'user has 450k transferable points', this command answers the multi-step optimization in one call instead of N searches.

novel_features.name: Effective-cost ranking via transfer ratios
novel_features.description: Given a transferable points program (Chase UR, Amex MR, etc.) and a route, list every redemption ranked by source-program points spent — multiplying each offer's price by the real transfer ratio (1:1 instant for UR→United vs 0.333:1 up_to_72 for Marriott→United).
novel_features.rationale: Pointhound's per-source transferOptions data is unique among award-search tools. Joins it locally to flip the question from 'cheapest in destination miles' to 'cheapest in source points'.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents reasoning about 'cheapest redemption' should ask in user-input units, not airline-output units. This command does the math.

novel_features.name: Saved-route polling with delta exit code
novel_features.description: Register a route as a saved watch; subsequent runs poll Pointhound and exit with code 2 only when a new or cheaper deal appears since the last snapshot. Perfect for cron.
novel_features.rationale: Awards 'change constantly; book within 24-48h'. Pointhound's web alerts are account-gated and email-based; this is a cron-shaped CLI primitive.
novel_features.example: pointhound-pp-cli watch SFO LIS 2026-08-15 --cabin business --quiet && say 'new deal'
novel_features.why_it_matters: The agent-native equivalent of 'tell me when something changes' — exit-code-driven, suitable for any scheduler.

novel_features.name: Fan-out search and store
novel_features.description: Issue N route+date searches in parallel from a CSV file (or repeated --route flags); all results are snapshotted to the local SQLite store with throttling.
novel_features.rationale: Replaces the typical Sunday-morning 'rerun the same 5 routes' ritual with one cron-able invocation. Pairs with watch and drift.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Multi-search is the common case for travel planning; web UIs only do one search at a time.

novel_features.name: Top Deals matrix with persistence
novel_features.description: Submit a multi-origin × multi-destination × month-range matrix search (e.g. SFO,LAX → LIS,FCO,LHR across Oct-Dec) and snapshot every result. Mirrors Pointhound's Premium Top Deals product but with offline access and cabin filtering.
novel_features.rationale: Pointhound's Top Deals search creates a single matrix search; this command fans that into the local store so cabin filtering and historical comparison are available without Premium.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Travel agents and trip planners can ask 'best Europe deal this fall?' in one shot.

novel_features.name: Cross-snapshot drift report
novel_features.description: For a watched route, diff the latest snapshot against the previous and show per-offer status: new, cheaper, disappeared, unchanged. Includes the points delta and timestamp gap.
novel_features.rationale: Pointhound's web UI is stateless — every search is fresh. Drift detection is something only a CLI with a local store can do.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Answers 'did anything change?' in one terse output, which is what an agent or human re-checker actually wants.

novel_features.name: Deal-rating explore via Scout
novel_features.description: Use Pointhound's `scout.pointhound.com/places/search` `dealRating` and `isTracked` fields to discover airports near a metro that historically have high-frequency deals, optionally chaining into `batch` to fetch live offers for them.
novel_features.rationale: Scout's deal-aware autocomplete is unique data; Pointhound's own UI uses it inline only — no standalone 'where do deals come from near me?' view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets an agent narrow the search space before fan-out: 'find me cheap deals from somewhere near NYC' becomes one command, not three.

novel_features.name: Best-month heatmap
novel_features.description: For a route + cabin, batch-search every month over a 12-month window and produce a month-grid showing min points cost per month (and the offer that achieved it).
novel_features.rationale: Award availability is seasonal; the 'best month' question requires fan-out and groupby that no single API call answers.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Trip-planning agents need a month picker, not a date picker, when the user says 'sometime next year'.

novel_features.name: Transfer-source lookup
novel_features.description: Given an airline redeem program (e.g. United MileagePlus), list every transferable earn program that feeds it with the ratio and transfer time (instant vs up_to_72).
novel_features.rationale: Companion to compare-transfer from the opposite direction. Surfaces Pointhound's unique transferOptions data as a reference table.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Quick lookup for 'can I get to United via Capital One?' without remembering the table.

narrative.headline: Every Pointhound flight search, plus a local SQLite of every deal you've ever seen, balance-aware reachability, and drift detection no other award-search tool has.
narrative.value_prop: Pointhound's web search is great for one-off lookups but doesn't compound. This CLI lets you batch search 20 routes overnight, ask 'where can I go with the points I actually have' in one call, watch a route and exit-code-2 when a new deal appears, and pivot every snapshot through agent-native --json output.
narrative.auth_narrative: Most of the CLI works anonymously — flight offer reads, filter facets, airport autocomplete, credit-card catalog. Only the `top-deals-matrix` and `search` commands need authentication, which happens via `pointhound-pp-cli auth login --chrome` — the CLI reads your existing Pointhound login cookies from Chrome (cf_clearance + ph_session) so no separate token is needed.

quickstart.comment: Find airport codes with Pointhound's deal-aware autocomplete (no auth).

quickstart.command: pointhound-pp-cli offers list --search-id ofs_xxx --cabins business --sort-by points --json --select results.data.id,results.data.pricePoints,results.data.airlinesList,results.data.totalDuration,results.data.totalStops
quickstart.comment: Read the offers for a search session you obtained from the website (anonymous).
Verify CLI health and connectivity to Pointhound. Run `auth login --chrome` once if you plan to use search creation or top-deals-matrix.
The headline novel command: where can I go in business this October?

troubleshoots.fix: Run `pointhound-pp-cli auth login --chrome` to import Pointhound cookies from your Chrome profile.
The search session expired. Get a fresh `ofs_*` from the Pointhound website URL, or run `pointhound-pp-cli search` after `auth login --chrome`.
Run `pointhound-pp-cli sync` to populate the local store with transferOptions; without it, balance math has no ratios to use.
Increase `--throttle` (default 1s) and re-run; batch uses adaptive backoff but will surface a typed rate-limit error if exhausted.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI whenever you need to compound award-search work: comparing multiple routes, tracking deals over time, asking balance-aware questions, or wiring Pointhound into a daily cron. The web UI is fine for one-off searches but loses everything when you close the tab; this CLI keeps every snapshot in a local SQLite for SQL queries, drift detection, and cross-route ranking.

recipes.title: Where can I fly in business this fall with my Chase UR?
recipes.explanation: from-home does the transfer-ratio math locally; --select narrows the response to four fields so an agent doesn't drown in offer detail.

recipes.title: Watch a tough route for a new deal
recipes.command: pointhound-pp-cli watch SFO HND 2026-12-22 --search-id ofs_xxx --cabin business --quiet
recipes.explanation: watch exits 2 only when something changes; chain it via && in your shell to fire a notification only on actual news. Wire into launchd or cron.

recipes.title: What did SFO-LIS look like last week vs today?
recipes.explanation: drift inspects the latest watch snapshot for a route; pair with `pointhound-pp-cli watch ...` runs over time.

recipes.title: Best month to fly SFO-NRT in business
recipes.explanation: Run one search per month-end first (or after `auth login --chrome`, use `top-deals-matrix` to populate), then point calendar at the resulting search ids.

recipes.title: Which earn programs feed Aeroplan?
recipes.explanation: Local lookup of Pointhound's transferOptions catalog for any redeem program present in the search. No web equivalent.

trigger_phrases: redeem credit card points for flight
where can I fly with my points
